prevalence.value: very low currently; sporadic transmission in some years
prevalence.trend: decreasing since the 2015-2016 epidemic; occasional regional clusters

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; concentrated burden via congenital Zika syndrome
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / PAHO

riskFactors: travel to areas with active transmission
lack of vector control and bite prevention
vulnerablePopulations: women of reproductive age in endemic areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: RT-PCR on serum/urine early; serology with cross-reactivity considerations; surveillance definitions per Ministério da Saúde

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; testing focused on pregnant people with compatible illness and congenital syndrome surveillance
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Supportive care only; prevention via vector control and reproductive health guidance

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Enhanced congenital syndrome surveillance and targeted maternal testing in SUS when indicated
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; pregnancy counseling; multidisciplinary follow-up for congenital Zika syndrome
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Diagnostic cross-reactivity with dengue and the long-term care burden for affected children and families
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established arbovirus surveillance infrastructure and congenital syndrome follow-up networks developed post-epidemic
